pages.name: dump
topLevelNames: Available Funds: The amount of money you have available to make the credit card payment is a significant factor. If you have sufficient funds in your bank account or cash on hand, you may be more likely to make the payment. Payment Due Date: The due date specified on your credit card statement influences when you make the payment. Many individuals prefer to pay off their credit card balances in full by the due date to avoid late payment fees and interest charges. Financial Management: Personal financial management practices play a role in credit card payment decisions. Some individuals may have a budgeting system in place and prioritize making timely credit card payments to maintain a good credit history and manage their overall debt. Interest Charges: Credit card holders who carry a balance from one billing cycle to the next incur interest charges. This factor may motivate individuals to make higher or additional payments to reduce their outstanding balance and minimize interest costs. Credit Score and History: Making consistent, on-time credit card payments contributes to a positive credit history and can help improve or maintain a good credit score. Individuals who value their creditworthiness may prioritize making credit card payments promptly. Rewards and Incentives: Credit cards often come with rewards programs or incentives such as cashback, travel points, or discounts. Individuals may consider their accrued rewards or the opportunity to earn more rewards as a motivating factor in making credit card payments. Financial Goals: Personal financial goals, such as reducing debt, saving for a specific purchase, or building an emergency fund, can influence credit card payment decisions. Individuals may allocate a portion of their funds towards achieving these goals, including making credit card payments accordingly.

Look for recurring issues, positive feedback, or specific suggestions and recommendations shared by respondents. Identify Trends and Patterns: Look for trends and patterns that emerge from the feedback analysis. This could involve identifying frequently mentioned positive aspects, common pain points, or areas where improvements are suggested. Pay attention to both positive and negative feedback to gain a comprehensive understanding of customers' experiences. Extract Key Insights: Based on the analysis, extract key insights from the survey feedback. Summarize the main findings, highlighting significant trends, patterns, and areas of concern or opportunities for improvement. These insights can be used to inform decision-making, product/service enhancements, or future strategies related to credit card usage. Step 7: Compare with Benchmarks or Previous Data: Comparing the survey feedback with industry benchmarks, it was found that the satisfaction rate with the rewards program was higher than the industry average, indicating a competitive advantage. However, the concerns regarding interest rates were consistent with industry trends. Sentiment Analysis: If the survey includes open-ended responses, conduct sentiment analysis to gauge the overall sentiment expressed by respondents. Use natural language processing techniques to categorize responses as positive, negative, or neutral. Identify common themes, issues, or suggestions emerging from the sentiment analysis.

pages.name: dump
topLevelNames: I’m really excited to talk about Net Promoter Score today, I think that most of you guys are familiar with NPS, so we’re gonna do a deep dive into NPS report for Atome app First of all, I will do a quick comparison of new users’ NPS score and repeat users’ NPS score from March 14th to June 14th this year. Then we will move on to get to know more about repeat users. And finally, I will give you some insight about what I’ve observed from the repeat users’ NPS survey results, and we’re going to compare new users and repeat users from a different perspective

Let me start by giving you some background information. What makes the report really interesting is that this is actually our 1st time collecting repeat users’ data. Just to clarify, Repeat Users are those who have purchased 3 times or more from Atome app. A new user is someone who placed just 1 order after they download the app. Let’s take a look at the right side first, we received 377 responses from Repeat Users in total, and the survey result shows that 59% of respondents are Promoters, 25% are Passives and 14% are Detractors. In that case, the NPS score for Repeat Users would be roughly 45. By comparison with the NPS score of Repeat Users, New Users obviously have relatively lower NPS A company’s NPS score can be range from - 100 to 100, the closer to 100, the better. Generally speaking, If the NPS is higher than 30 that would indicate that the product is doing Great. It’s seems like we are on the right path, we have a lot more happy users than unhappy ones

As I mentioned earlier, we received 377 responses from our repeat users, it can be difficult for us to quickly visualize what users are talking about. The world cloud definitely helps us to quickly highlight the most frequently mentioned words from open-ended questions. Typically, the bigger the word, the more frequently it appeared in the response. Looking at the result of the world cloud, words related to Bills/Payment and Incentives, got the most mentions

Let’s move on and look at the graph of overview of NPS issue type We have over 94% respondents who are willing to share their experience with us. Based on the findings, about 32 respondents complain about problems related to Bills/Payment, For example, one of the Detractors said that she felt discouraged to use Atome because she was charged a $15 late fee and her account was frozen right away for just missed 1 day late payment   On top of that, the data shows that Bills/Payment, Incentive and Customer Service are the main factors impact the NPS based on a number of times the issue was mentioned by respondents

The table listed all the key pain points faced by each categories based on their score and responses. Both Promoters and Passives are dissatisfied with the type issues like Voucher and Tenor(multi). Despite Detractors are the exact opposite of promoters, it turns out that they both complain about the limit of credit In addition, I breakdown the keywords and list some valuable suggestions which urgently need improvement

Detractors are unhappy users who give a score of 6 or below. What is interesting to note is that an user that gives a rating of 6 for the NPS survey does not have the same mindset as an user that gives a 0. And yet, both of these users put into the same category. For this reason, I decide to further divide detractors into 2 categories and want to find out the difference between them. I break down the group into moderate detractors (rating of 4 to 6) and super-detractors (rating of 0 to 3). On the right side, it’s very clearly to see that the Top 5 most disappointing issues are Credit Limit, Customer Service, Fees, Bugs and Payment/card. More specifically, I notice that there are many Detractors who complain about credit limit, they give a very low score and some of them even mention other competitors who offer higher credit limit than us But overall, I think priority will be given to addressing the super-detractors’s problems, because they are very dangerous for business and may even discourage others from using Atome app

Passives are users who give a score of 7 or 8. The Passives are tricky because they are indifferent and could become promoters — or they could easily switch to competitors, depending on future product experiences

I also divide Passives into 2 groups, and i notice that Voucher, Feature, Tenor(multi) and Payment/card are top issues that matters to them most. For instance, vouchers-related problems mentioned more frequently. for example, some of the Passives mentioned that the voucher is not refunded even if her order is cancelled We have over 25% passives, this is actually not a small number. By focusing on that 25% that are passively engaging with Atome app, we probably can gain some promoters in the process if we can make efforts to make them feel heard and valued.

Let's zoom in on the analysis of Repeat Users particularly. This stacked bar chart shows the trend in the number of promoters, passives and detractors by month. The solid blue line shows us the actual NPS result for each month. Over the whole time period, I have 2 key findings from chart. first, April has the highest NPS with score of 51.28. second, we see a slight decrease in Promoters in May For the first finding, I can only think of two reasons behind it. One reason could be that some new features launched and another one could be that a seasonal marketing campaign was running during the time And then, we actually talked to the Marketing Team and they confirmed that my guess was right, they told us that they launched a series of advertising and campaign for Hari Raya celebration in SG from March to April On top of that, more and more merchants started to partner with Atome, especially there’re some big-name companies like flash coffee. In my opinion, being active in local events can definitely help Atome to gain more exposure and may have some impact on improving NPS score

After analyzing the NPS survey results, I’m sure that we all have a better understanding of Repeat Users So now we can go back and make a comparison again with New Users. As we can see, there are some similarities between new users and repeat users, they both still have trouble using vouchers and have disappointing experience with Customer Service however, they have their own total different top complaints. For new users, they feel dissatisfied about not able to pay full amount. Repeat Users focus more on Credit limit & Installment period. For example, they want to check credit limit and balance on App, and also they want to get their credit limit increase, and so on. These are some observation I gained from NPS analysis, I hope this will be helpful to you

pages.name: Page 1
topLevelNames: 分期意愿低？WHY？ 提高分期意愿 → 提供多种installment program？

Many Filipinos remain unbanked or without access to credit cards / formal credit products — nearly half the population still lacks formal banking/credit access, which limits eligibility for installment plans. High distrust of credit risks — concerns about high interest rates, fraud, or unstable income make people reluctant to commit to multi‑month payments. Alternative financing/payment methods (e‑wallets, short‑term financing, BNPL or cash‑based purchases) remain more accessible or better aligned with many Filipinos’ income/comfort level than long‑term installments.

A 2025 study cited by RCBC / Mastercard estimates that ≈ 49.8% of Filipino adults did not have a bank account in 2024. As of 2024–2025, roughly 50 % of Filipino adults are unbanked — meaning many don’t have bank accounts or (likely) credit cards, which limits how many people can use traditional card‑installment/transactional‑installment programs.

pages.name: Page 1
topLevelNames: Facebook 横向视频 最佳尺寸：1280 x 720 像素 比例：16：9 最大文件：4GB 格式：MP4 MOV 最大时长：120分钟 最大帧数：30fps
Instagram 横向、方形或垂直 横向最低分辨率：600 x 315 方形：600 x 600 垂直：600 x 750 横向比例：16：9 方形：1：1 垂直：4：5 最大文件：4GB 格式：MP4 MOV 最大帧数30fps
Tiktok 垂直、方形 尺寸：1080×1920 横向比例：9：16（普通智能手机全屏） 或1：1（不会是全屏） 格式：MP4 MOV
Twitter 横向 垂直 最佳横向尺寸：320 x 180 像素、640 x 360 像素和 1280 x 720 像素。 可能的最大尺寸：1280 x 1024 像素 Video format for desktop: .MP4. Video format for mobile .MOV. Video maximum frames are 40fps.
Pinterest Video Length 方形，垂直 推荐尺寸：600 x 600 像素 垂直视频尺寸：600 x 900 像素 方形纵横比：1:1。 Pinterest 垂直纵横比：9:16
LinkedIn 纵横比：在 1:2.4 和 2.4:1 之间
Snapchat 推荐尺寸：1080 x 1920 像素 纵横比：9:16 长篇 Snapchat 视频广告 纵横比：9:16 或 16:9 视频格式：.MP4 和 .MOV
